title: Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma
what_description: Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma is a cacti subspecies within the Cactaceae family, characterized by its spherical to oblate stems, reaching diameters of 10-15 cm, and heights of 3-6 cm, with 22-30 radial and 4-5 central spines, the latter often reaching 5 cm in length. The stems are covered with fine, white, hair-like areoles and have 5-6 ribs, each with a prominent tubercle, giving a warty appearance. The flowers are funnel-shaped, pale yellow to brownish, 3.5-4.5 cm in length, and 2.5-3.5 cm in width, with a short, white, woolly perianth. Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma occurs in the Mexican states of San Luis Potosí and Chihuahua, typically in desert and scrub habitats at elevations of 1,500-2,000 meters. This subspecies is distinguished from its parent species by its more compact and rounded stems.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma are skin inflammation, wound healing, skin irritation and skin issues, all attributed to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. Additionally, this plant has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against fungal infections, making it a potential treatment for various diseases. Its pain-relieving properties also make it useful for treating pain, and its antimicrobial and antibacterial properties render it effective against bacterial infections. Furthermore, the plant contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which is beneficial for conditions such as hypertension and diabetes. Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma has also been found to exhibit antiseptic activity against wounds, burns, acne, eczema, gastroenteritis, and insect bites, while its antibacterial and antifungal properties also make it effective against bacterial infections and fungal infections.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma are rooted in its antioxidant activity, which helps protect the body from oxidative damage caused by free radicals. Its antimicrobial properties, particularly against bacteria and fungi, are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and volatile oils that inhibit the growth of microorganisms. Additionally, the plant exhibits anti-inflammatory activity due to the presence of saponins and other compounds that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Furthermore, the bioactive compounds of Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma also possess antifungal properties, making it effective against fungal infections. The analgesic properties of the plant, resulting from the combination of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, provide relief from pain and discomfort.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma are indole alkaloids, saponins, glycosides, flavonoids, and N-methyltryptamine. These bioactive compounds have been found to possess various medicinal properties, with indole alkaloids exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Saponins, on the other hand, have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties, while glycosides have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Flavonoids, a group of plant-derived compounds, have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties. N-methyltryptamine, a tryptamine alkaloid, has been found to possess antidepressant and analgesic activities.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma are coryphantha tincture, which is a concentrated liquid extract typically made with ethanol or glycerin to preserve its medicinal properties, bumamma salve, a topical preparation used to treat skin issues, made by infusing the plant in oil and mixing it with beeswax or other emollients to create a protective barrier, tea, which is an aqueous infusion used for its potential medicinal effects, and infused oil, a liquid preparation used to deliver the plant's nutrients and properties directly to the skin, while an infusion is a liquid preparation used for its potential medicinal effects, all of which are made from the plant due to its purported antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Coryphantha elephantidens subsp. bumamma improperly include experiencing nausea, which can be caused by the presence of toxic alkaloids such as glochidion and scilliroside. Developing diarrhea can also occur, as the plant's sap can irritate the gastrointestinal tract, leading to water loss and electrolyte imbalance. Experiencing vomiting is another potential side effect, which can exacerbate dehydration and electrolyte imbalances. Nausea, along with these symptoms, can be severe enough to lead to dehydration, electrolyte imbalance, and potentially life-threatening complications. These side effects can occur if the plant's sap is ingested, inhaled, or comes into contact with mucous membranes.
